discovery.text: From Allan B. Jacobs' Great Streets (1993). Madrid's great early 20th-century boulevard — cut through the old city fabric in three phases from 1910-1952, creating a Spanish version of Haussmann.

narrative.summary: Madrid's great early 20th-century boulevard — cut through the old city fabric in three phases from 1910-1952, creating a Spanish version of Haussmann.

blocks.title: Why Jacobs Chose It
blocks.body: Grand early modernist buildings (Edificio Metrópolis, the Telefónica tower), continuous street wall, wide sidewalks, lively mixed use from cinemas to rooftop bars. The rooftop terraces (terrazas) are the real summer destination — bars on top of the buildings looking out over the city.

blocks.title: How to Walk It
blocks.body: Walk the full length. Jacobs is explicit: you have to walk a great street to understand it — the sequence, the proportions, how the edges change. Give it an hour minimum. Sit at a café or on a bench midway. Notice what makes it feel alive.
